body_txt: The Sitcom Arch-Nemesis is an alternative to the traditional Arch-Enemy , generally Played for Laughs . They usually are not dangerous or evil, but instead are defined primarily by how they just rub a more prominent character from the show entirely the wrong way, and the two will never miss an opportunity to have at each other with Volleying Insults or small-minded pranks . It's possible they are The Friend Nobody Likes , but it's just as possible they are a straight-up Nice Guy who acts as a foil to the character who despises them. Either way, they inspire rage upon even hearing their name , essentially being their "favorite enemy" in a sense. Often, the two are kindred spirits and don't realize it; these rivals are much more likely to have an Enemy Mine plot than dramatic rivals. Since generally there isn't much at stake in that rivalry, it will usually revolve around insignificant, everyday matters, filled with comedic moments. Also given the humor inherent in the relationship, any interaction between such rivals works better with bickering and petty insults rather than any legitimately hurtful remarks and the emotions behind it are funnier if they remain not all-too-serious wrath and do not sour into outright hate. Alternatively, the rival may turn out to be a Romantic False Lead for the primary love interest, and the Love Triangle is the core source of their rivalry. The Sitcom Arch-Nemesis is usually just a recurring character, because if they were part of the main cast then the constant fighting would take over the show. Indeed, often they are oblivious to this rivalry or never seen in person , further defining them entirely by how the other character views them. Note that these aren't necessarily unique to sitcoms, and other stories – including more comedic action stories – can have a character who fills this role, sometimes even if there is a character who fulfils the role of an actual Arch-Enemy . In the more lighthearted action shows, particularly in certain cartoons and kid shows, sometimes the actual Arch-Enemy will act like a Sitcom Arch-Nemesis when the story or comedy demands it, alternating between attempting to Take Over the World one day, trying to wreck the hero's birthday party the next , and maybe even Go-Karting with Bowser in-between. Compare with The Rival and Vitriolic Best Buds . Often used with Comically Lopsided Rivalry .
